question_title: Will Yuma, Arizona receive any rain in April 2026?
summary: Yuma, AZ is the driest city in the US (~3 inches annual rainfall). April 1-12, 2026 have been completely dry (0.00 precip confirmed from the resolution source). The NWS 7-day forecast and AccuWeather 10-day forecast both show 0% precipitation through April 22. Historical analysis of 12 years (2014-2025) shows that in 9 years where the first 12 April days were dry, only 1 year (2019, with rain on days 16 and 22) had material precipitation afterward — a conditional rate of 11.1%. With forecasts extending through April 22, the effective uncertainty window is reduced to just April 23-30 (~8 days), and 0/9 historical years had rain after day 22 conditional on a dry start. I estimate ~10% probability, slightly above the pure historical conditioning to account for the small sample size and genuine uncertainty beyond the forecast window.
reasoning:
**Forecast: 10% probability (YES)**

**Key findings:**
- **April 1-12 confirmed dry** (0.00 precip every day from the resolution source)
- **10-day forecast completely dry** through April 22 (NWS + AccuWeather both show 0% precip)
- **Historical conditional rate**: Of 9 years where the first 12 April days were dry at this station, only 1 (2019) had material rain afterward — an 11.1% rate
- **Narrowed uncertainty window**: With forecasts covering through Apr 22, only ~8 unforecast days remain (Apr 23-30). Zero of 9 comparable historical years had rain after day 22
- **Extreme aridity**: Yuma averages ~3 inches/year total; April is one of its driest months

The 10% estimate balances the very strong evidence for continued dryness against the genuine uncertainty from the small historical sample and the 8-day window beyond forecast coverage.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Yuma, Arizona receive any rain in April 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43138)
[Yuma, AZ Weather Forecast | Local Weather Updates for Arizona by FOX Weather](https://www.foxweather.com/local-weather/arizona/yuma)
resolution_criteria:
This question resolves as Yes if according to the data calendar for the National Weather Service's Yuma International Airport weather station, there is any material precipitation observed for any day in April 2026: https://mesonet.agron.iastate.edu/sites/hist.phtml?station=NYL&network=AZ_ASOS

For purposes of this question, days with a recording of 0.00 inches, Trace, and M (missing) will all be considered to have had no material precipitation.
